source.title: Hippocampal replay in the awake state: a potential substrate for memory consolidation and retrieval.

confidence_rationale: Authoritative review synthesising evidence that awake replay during brief immobility has dual roles: consolidation (compressed sequence replay supports downstream Hebbian plasticity) and retrieval (reactivates prior trajectories for forward planning). The immobility-triggered nature directly maps onto MECH-092's quiescent E3 heartbeat window. Confidence 0.70 reflects review status (no new primary data) and the moderate inference required to map consolidation/retrieval distinction onto REE's architectural separation between offline MECH-092 cycles and waking ARC-028 gate release.
failure_signatures: As a review, the paper synthesises findings from multiple tasks and laboratories. The proposed dual-role interpretation (consolidation + retrieval) is the authors' theoretical framing, not a direct experimental test.

The remote-environment representation finding (replay of trajectories from environments not currently occupied) is described but not mechanistically explained -- it is unclear whether this requires a distinct circuit or is a natural consequence of the generative nature of replay.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Awake hippocampal replay occurs during brief pauses in behavior; can represent trajectories through current or spatially remote environments; serves dual roles in consolidation (offline Hebbian strengthening at compressed timescale) and retrieval (reactivating stored trajectories for planning); sensory context modulates replay content.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-028 and MECH-092 map onto the two roles Carr et al. describe. Waking replay at trajectory completion (ARC-028) corresponds to the consolidation/evaluation role: the completion event (reward receipt or goal arrival) triggers a brief replay burst that updates the hippocampal map and (via dopamine) releases BetaGate for the next action selection cycle. MECH-092 (quiescent E3 heartbeat replay) corresponds to the retrieval role: during behavioral pauses, the hippocampus actively retrieves and evaluates possible future trajectories from remote environments. The key architectural implication: both modes use the same SWR-replay substrate but are triggered by different conditions (endpoint PE vs. quiescent heartbeat).
mapping.mapping_caveat: In biology, consolidation and retrieval may be mediated by the same SWR mechanism operating on the same sequence representations. In REE, ARC-028 and MECH-092 are architecturally distinct (one is a gate release signal, the other is an offline replay cycle). Whether these should eventually be unified into a single substrate (one SWR-equivalent module operating in different modes) is an open question for V4.
mapping.source_context: Review spanning rodent electrophysiology studies (linear tracks, mazes, multiple environments). Awake replay reviewed distinct from sleep replay.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high (Nature Neuroscience review). Mapping fidelity moderate -- the dual-role framing is directly useful but is theoretical interpretation of primary data. Transfer risk moderate: the awake/quiescent distinction maps well to REE's design, but the remote-environment finding requires REE to have multi-environment maps (not yet implemented in V3).
